title: cornus mas f. variegata
intro: Cornus mas f. variegata, also known as the Variegated Dogwood or Cornelian Cherry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The bark, leaves, and berries of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Cornus mas f. variegata include reducing fever, relieving coughs and sore throats, and providing antioxidant and anti-aging properties. In herbal preparations, the bark and berries are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
